commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fcabf1146394f5c5ddc5fec7f2e9042b9e15d10f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a full-CLI shutdown step that cancels active deferred context-engine turn-maintenance runs, clears their lanes, and threads cancellation into maintenance calls.

PR surface: Source +141, Tests +174. Total +315 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. Source inspection shows current main can schedule background turn maintenance without a full-CLI exit cleanup path, but the PR's own live command timed out before observing successful result-plus-exit behavior.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer/API review for the public context-engine cancellation shape; no ClawSweeper repair lane should bypass that proof gate.

target.title: fix(cli): cancel deferred maintenance on exit
target.body:
## Problem

Some short-lived `openclaw agent --local` commands can finish their foreground work while background context-engine maintenance is still queued or running. In that state the command has no more user-visible work to do, but the background maintenance path can keep the Node process alive and make operators wait for an outer wrapper timeout.

## Change and value

This PR adds a targeted CLI teardown step for full Commander-based CLI commands. After `program.parseAsync()` returns, the CLI now cancels active deferred turn-maintenance runs, clears their queue lanes, and gives them a short best-effort drain before the rest of process cleanup continues.

The value is narrower than a global exit workaround: completed short-lived CLI commands can release known deferred context-engine work without calling `process.exit()` or changing the routed fast-path commands.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and scripts running short-lived full CLI commands, especially `openclaw agent --local`, where deferred context-engine maintenance can otherwise keep the process alive after command completion.
- Not affected: routed fast-path commands such as the lightweight `status` path. Those do not enter the full Commander parse path and do not run this cancellation hook.
- Not fixed here: cases where the agent command never resolves or its own timeout does not abort the run. That is a separate hang class and needs a separate PR.

## Why now

Local testing against the current CLI still shows a completed `agent --local` invocation can emit the expected JSON result and then remain alive until an external timeout kills it. This gives us a concrete cleanup boundary to fix while keeping the broader hard-timeout problem separate.

## Implementation

- Adds an optional `AbortSignal` to context-engine maintenance inputs and runtime context.
- Tracks active deferred turn-maintenance task/run ids so CLI shutdown can mark queued or active work as cancelled instead of letting stale workers overwrite terminal task state.
- Clears and resets the affected turn-maintenance command lanes during CLI completion.
- Runs the cancellation only after full CLI parsing was entered, so routed commands keep their existing lightweight behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Completed short-lived CLI commands can be held open by queued or active deferred context-engine maintenance after user-visible work finishes.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux/WSL from `openclaw/openclaw` main at `ec168fa2bdda15be7430f41cb7ef0b610b6e68bf`, branch head `fcabf11463`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `timeout 120s pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent cm-pi-off --timeout 45 --message 'Reply exactly CLI_EXIT_PROBE_OK' --json`

Evidence after fix: The live command did not reach a successful JSON result on this branch; it timed out before completion with `[ELIFECYCLE] Command failed.`

Observed result after fix: The focused deferred-maintenance cancellation behavior is covered by regression tests, but the real CLI proof is incomplete because the live command encountered the separate "agent promise never resolves / internal timeout does not hard-kill" failure class first.

What was not tested: A real end-to-end `agent --local` run that both emits a final result and then proves this deferred-maintenance cleanup lets the process exit without the local `cli-exit-fix` workaround. This PR is draft until that proof gap is either closed or maintainers accept the split and review it as a scoped cleanup.

## Verification

- `src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts`: 89/89 passed
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`: 21/21 passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1`
- `git diff --check source-origin/main`

## Out of scope

The command-level hard-timeout problem is intentionally separate. The follow-up PR should handle the case where `agent --local --timeout N` does not cause the command promise to resolve and the process stays alive until an outer `timeout` wrapper kills it.

AI-assisted: yes.
target.author: kaspre
